refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall enable execution of the load→travel→dump cycle within the customer-defined cycle time target.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR conflates two distinct obligations: (1) supporting/enabling the capability to achieve cycle time, and (2) actually achieving the measured KPI. The refined FR is atomic and solution-neutral by focusing on the functional obligation—enabling the cycle to execute within the time envelope—without prescribing how (e.g., via speed optimization, path planning, operator training, or equipment design). The actual achievement of p95 ≤ CT_target is a verification criterion, not a functional requirement. This separation allows DPs to address enablement independently of operational performance measurement.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Demonstrate that under nominal duty cycle and site conditions, the NGHE solution's design permits completion of load→travel→dump within CT_target. Measure p95 cycle time across representative worksite trials; if p95 ≤ CT_target, the FR is satisfied. If p95 > CT_target, investigate whether the failure is due to (a) inadequate DP (design does not enable the speed/efficiency needed) or (b) operational/environmental factors outside the DP scope.
sub_frs: FR-NGHE-002.1: The NGHE solution shall enable load acquisition at the designated load point.
FR-NGHE-002.2: The NGHE solution shall enable travel from load point to dump point.
FR-NGHE-002.3: The NGHE solution shall enable material discharge at the designated dump point.
FR-NGHE-002.4: The NGHE solution shall minimize idle/transition time between load, travel, and dump phases.

candidate_dps: DP1: Decompose cycle into discrete, sequenced phases (load, travel, dump) with phase-gated transitions and independent phase duration targets that sum to CT_target
DP2: Implement real-time cycle monitoring with feedback control to dynamically adjust phase execution rates and maintain p95 ≤ CT_target under variable site conditions
DP3: Pre-calculate and store optimal phase timing profiles (load duration, travel speed, dump duration) as lookup tables indexed by payload mass and site terrain, selected at cycle start
DP4: Allocate fixed time budgets to each phase with safety margins, enforce hard phase deadlines via watchdog timers, and log phase overruns for root cause analysis

DP5: Integrate duty cycle simulation into design validation; measure actual cycle times under representative worksite conditions (payload range, terrain, ambient) and tune phase parameters to achieve p95 ≤ CT_target before deployment
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP1 + DP5 (Composite): Decompose the load→travel→dump cycle into independently-timed phases with explicit duration targets for each phase that collectively satisfy CT_target. Validate the decomposition and phase timing via duty cycle simulation under agreed site conditions (payload range, terrain, ambient) to confirm p95 ≤ CT_target before production release.

potential_sources: Phase sequencing: load→travel→dump order is fixed by operational logic; no coupling risk.
Phase duration interdependence: Each phase has an independent duration target; phase timing is not coupled to other phases' execution (e.g., load time does not depend on travel speed).
Site condition variability: DP5 (duty cycle simulation) explicitly accounts for payload, terrain, and ambient variation; phase targets are tuned to handle the agreed range, reducing runtime coupling.
Safety margin allocation: DP4 (watchdog timers and overrun logging) decouples phase execution from external disturbances by enforcing hard deadlines and capturing deviations for offline analysis.

Feedback control (DP2) rejected: Dynamic rate adjustment during cycle execution introduces closed-loop coupling between phases and real-time sensor feedback; deferred to later design phases if p95 target cannot be met with DP1+DP5.
coupling_check.notes: DP1+DP5 achieves low coupling by treating each phase as an independent execution unit with pre-validated timing targets. Coupling is deferred to the implementation layer (IM), where phase orchestration logic and sensor integration will be packaged. No functional coupling between phases is introduced at the DP level.

assumptions_risks: ASSUMPTION: Cycle time variability is primarily driven by payload mass and site terrain (not random disturbances or equipment degradation). RISK: If unmodeled variability sources exist (e.g., hydraulic fluid temperature drift, wear), p95 target may not be achievable with static phase timing. MITIGATION: Extend duty cycle simulation to include thermal and wear scenarios; consider DP2 (feedback control) if residual variability exceeds acceptable margin.

ASSUMPTION: Phase duration targets can be independently validated and tuned without cross-phase interaction. RISK: If phase execution times are coupled (e.g., load time affects travel speed due to payload inertia), DP1 decomposition may be invalid. MITIGATION: Perform coupled system analysis during DP→IM mapping to identify and model phase interactions.

ASSUMPTION: Worksite safety plan and operational policies do not impose hard constraints on phase execution (e.g., mandatory idle time between phases, speed limits during travel). RISK: If safety policies introduce fixed overhead, CT_target may be unachievable. MITIGATION: Validate CT_target against safety policy constraints before design; revise CT_target or safety policy if conflict exists.

ASSUMPTION: Duty cycle simulation can accurately represent site conditions (payload range, terrain, ambient). RISK: If simulation is inaccurate or incomplete, p95 validation may fail in production. MITIGATION: Conduct field trials on representative worksites; compare simulated vs. measured cycle times; update simulation model based on field data.

ASSUMPTION: Phase timing targets are achievable with available actuation and control technology. RISK: If phase targets are too aggressive (e.g., load time < minimum hydraulic cycle time), DP1 is infeasible. MITIGATION: Perform feasibility check during DP→IM mapping; confirm that each phase target is achievable with candidate actuators and control strategies.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: FR (refined): The NGHE solution shall enable execution of the load→travel→dump cycle such that the measured p95 cycle time under the agreed duty cycle (payload range, site terrain, ambient conditions) and worksite safety policies does not exceed CT_target. RATIONALE: Explicit inclusion of duty cycle definition, site conditions, and safety policy constraints in the FR clarifies the scope and acceptance criteria for DP validation. This revision supports traceability from FR to DP5 (duty cycle simulation) and reduces ambiguity in the p95 measurement protocol.

im_boundary.name: Vehicle
im_boundary.scope: Autonomous cycle planning, phase decomposition, duty cycle simulation, and timing validation for load→travel→dump operations under site-specific conditions

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Vehicle reports actual phase durations, cycle times, payload mass, terrain classification, and ambient conditions to enable post-hoc validation and drift detection

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Vehicle receives site conditions (payload range, terrain profile, ambient parameters) and CT_target to parameterize phase decomposition and simulation

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: Vehicle signals if simulated or observed p95 cycle time exceeds CT_target, triggering safety review before production release

module_containment_scores.notes: Vehicle owns cycle planning, phase timing logic, and duty cycle simulation. Payload, terrain, and ambient data are vehicle-intrinsic or sensor-derived. Phase decomposition and validation are core to autonomous operation. Minor diffusion: CT_target and site conditions arrive via Tasking (external input); validation results must propagate to Safety Services via SafetyAlert.

NGHE is a system-level orchestrator. Cycle planning and phase timing are vehicle-specific concerns; placing them at NGHE level creates tight coupling to vehicle internals and diffuses phase logic across multiple vehicles if fleet heterogeneity exists. Simulation and validation would require NGHE to model each vehicle variant, violating separation of concerns.

Teleoperation is a remote control interface for human operators. Autonomous cycle decomposition and duty cycle simulation are not teleoperation concerns. Placing DP1+DP5 here would require Teleoperation to embed vehicle dynamics, simulation, and timing logic—severe architectural violation. High diffusion to Vehicle and Safety Services.

Safety Services validates and enforces safety constraints (e.g., p95 ≤ CT_target). However, the cycle decomposition, phase timing, and simulation are design/planning activities, not safety enforcement. Placing DP1+DP5 here conflates design with validation. Diffusion: cycle logic must still reside in Vehicle; Safety Services becomes a bottleneck for every cycle variant.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The Vehicle module is the natural owner of cycle phase decomposition and duty cycle simulation because: (1) it encapsulates the physical/kinematic constraints (payload capacity, terrain interaction, travel speed, dump mechanism timing) that determine phase durations; (2) it has direct access to sensor data (payload mass, terrain classification, ambient conditions) needed to parameterize simulation; (3) autonomous cycle planning is a core Vehicle responsibility; (4) phase timing validation is a prerequisite for Vehicle operational readiness. External interfaces (Tasking, Telemetry, SafetyAlert) provide clean boundaries for input (site conditions, CT_target), output (actual cycle metrics), and safety signaling.
containment_risks: Vehicle module may become large if it also owns low-level motor control, sensor fusion, and localization. Mitigation: decompose Vehicle into sub-modules (e.g., CycleController, DutyCycleSimulator, PhaseOrchestrator) with clear internal interfaces.

Simulation fidelity depends on accurate terrain and payload models. If these models are maintained externally (e.g., in NGHE or a shared database), Vehicle must import them via Tasking or a dedicated configuration interface. Risk: model drift or version mismatch. Mitigation: version-lock simulation models with each production release.

Site-specific conditions (payload range, terrain profile, ambient) are provided via Tasking. If these are incomplete or incorrect, simulation validation becomes unreliable. Mitigation: Vehicle must validate input completeness and raise SafetyAlert if conditions are out of bounds.

p95 validation is statistical; it requires sufficient operational data. Pre-production simulation may not capture all edge cases. Mitigation: implement continuous monitoring (Telemetry) post-release and trigger re-validation if observed p95 drifts above CT_target.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: DP1+DP5 is well-scoped and appropriately assigned to Vehicle. No revision needed. However, consider refining the DP to explicitly separate concerns: (1) DP1a: Decompose load→travel→dump cycle into independently-timed phases with explicit duration targets. (2) DP5a: Implement duty cycle simulator parameterized by site conditions (payload range, terrain, ambient). (3) DP5b: Validate phase timing via simulation and flag if p95 > CT_target. This separation allows Vehicle to own DP1a and DP5a (design and simulation), while Safety Services owns DP5b (validation gate). This preserves Vehicle's autonomy while giving Safety Services explicit veto authority.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: High. Acceptance criterion 'p95 ≤ CT_target under agreed duty cycle and site conditions' is directly measurable via Telemetry (actual phase durations, cycle times, payload mass, terrain classification, ambient conditions). Pre-production validation via duty cycle simulation under site-specific conditions is traceable and repeatable. SafetyAlert provides explicit gate signal before production release.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear. Vehicle module owns cycle decomposition, phase timing, and simulation validation. Tasking interface provides parameterization (site conditions, CT_target). Telemetry enables post-hoc verification. SafetyAlert enforces gate. Ownership boundary is well-defined with minimal ambiguity.

fr_dp_gate.reason: DP (DP1 + DP5 composite) directly addresses FR: phase decomposition with explicit duration targets + duty cycle simulation under site conditions collectively satisfy CT_target. Logic is sound and verifiable. Acceptance criterion 'p95 ≤ CT_target' is a direct output of the DP. No logical gaps detected.

diffusion_risks: Simulation model fidelity depends on external terrain and payload models. If these are maintained outside Vehicle (e.g., in NGHE or shared database), version mismatch or model drift could invalidate simulation results. Mitigation: version-lock models with production release; Vehicle must validate model currency via Tasking.

Site-specific conditions (payload range, terrain profile, ambient) are external inputs via Tasking. Incomplete or incorrect inputs will compromise simulation validity. Mitigation: Vehicle must validate input completeness and raise SafetyAlert if conditions are out of bounds or insufficient for reliable p95 estimation.

p95 validation is statistical and pre-production simulation may not capture all edge cases (e.g., extreme terrain, payload distribution outliers). Mitigation: implement continuous Telemetry monitoring post-release; trigger re-validation if observed p95 drifts above CT_target.

Vehicle module may become large if it also owns low-level motor control, sensor fusion, and localization. Risk: tight coupling and reduced modularity. Mitigation: decompose Vehicle into sub-modules (CycleController, DutyCycleSimulator, PhaseOrchestrator) with clear internal interfaces.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Vehicle is the correct owner (containment_score 0.92, diffusion_score 0.08). It encapsulates physical/kinematic constraints (payload capacity, terrain interaction, travel speed, dump timing) that determine phase durations. It has direct access to sensor data (payload mass, terrain classification, ambient). Autonomous cycle planning is a core Vehicle responsibility. External interfaces (Tasking, Telemetry, SafetyAlert) provide clean boundaries. Minor diffusion is acceptable: CT_target and site conditions are external inputs (Tasking); validation results propagate to Safety Services (SafetyAlert). This is appropriate separation of concerns, not architectural violation.

recommended_revisions: Refine DP to explicitly separate concerns: (1) DP1a: Decompose load→travel→dump cycle into independently-timed phases with explicit duration targets. (2) DP5a: Implement duty cycle simulator parameterized by site conditions (payload range, terrain, ambient). (3) DP5b: Validate phase timing via simulation and flag if p95 > CT_target. This allows Vehicle to own DP1a and DP5a (design and simulation), while Safety Services owns DP5b (validation gate). Preserves Vehicle autonomy while giving Safety Services explicit veto authority.

Add explicit input validation to Vehicle: Tasking interface must include schema validation. Vehicle must reject incomplete or out-of-bounds site conditions and raise SafetyAlert with diagnostic details.
Establish version-locking protocol for terrain and payload models used in simulation. Document model versions in Telemetry output to enable post-hoc traceability of simulation assumptions.

Define continuous monitoring strategy post-production release: Telemetry must track observed p95 cycle time against simulated p95. If observed p95 exceeds CT_target by threshold (e.g., 5%), trigger automated re-validation workflow and escalate to Safety Services.

Consider sub-module decomposition within Vehicle to manage complexity: CycleController (phase orchestration), DutyCycleSimulator (simulation engine), PhaseOrchestrator (timing logic). Define internal interfaces to prevent tight coupling.

decision.justification: All three gates (FR-DP, DP-IM, FR-IM) pass with high confidence. However, the recommended_revisions and diffusion_risks reveal that FR1 is still at a level of abstraction that masks critical sub-concerns: (1) phase decomposition logic (DP1a), (2) duty cycle simulation parameterization (DP5a), (3) validation gating (DP5b), and (4) input validation and model version management. These are not minor implementation details—they are distinct functional concerns that must be explicitly decomposed to ensure traceability, ownership clarity, and risk mitigation. The high safety criticality of this FR demands that we push decomposition down to expose these concerns as explicit sub-FRs, each with its own DP-IM mapping and verification strategy. Stopping here would leave critical validation and input-handling logic implicit, violating the principle of explicit architectural transparency required for safety-critical systems.
next_level_sub_frs: FR1.1: Decompose autonomous load-haul-dump cycle into independently-timed phases (load, travel, dump) with explicit duration targets derived from site conditions and vehicle constraints.
FR1.2: Simulate duty cycle performance under site-specific conditions (payload range, terrain profile, ambient conditions) to predict p95 cycle time before production release.
FR1.3: Validate that simulated p95 cycle time does not exceed CT_target; raise SafetyAlert if validation fails.

FR1.4: Validate completeness and correctness of site-condition inputs (payload range, terrain classification, ambient parameters) received from Tasking; reject out-of-bounds or insufficient inputs with diagnostic SafetyAlert.
FR1.5: Maintain version traceability of terrain and payload models used in duty cycle simulation; ensure model versions are locked with production release and documented in Telemetry output.
FR1.6: Monitor observed cycle time post-production via Telemetry; trigger re-validation workflow if observed p95 drifts above CT_target by defined threshold (e.g., 5%).

revise.why: Current DP (DP1 + DP5 composite) conflates multiple distinct design concerns. Refine to explicitly separate: (1) DP1a: Phase decomposition logic with duration targets. (2) DP5a: Duty cycle simulator parameterized by site conditions. (3) DP5b: Validation gate logic (p95 vs. CT_target). (4) DP5c: Input validation schema and rejection logic. (5) DP5d: Model version-locking and traceability mechanism. (6) DP5e: Continuous post-release monitoring and re-validation trigger. This refinement ensures each sub-FR has a distinct, traceable DP and allows ownership boundaries (Vehicle vs. Safety Services) to be explicitly assigned at the sub-FR level rather than implicitly at the composite level.

assumptions: Site-condition inputs (payload range, terrain profile, ambient) are provided by Tasking with sufficient fidelity and completeness to enable reliable p95 estimation.
Terrain and payload models used in duty cycle simulation are maintained with version control and can be locked to production releases.
Telemetry infrastructure can capture observed phase durations and cycle times with sufficient granularity to enable post-hoc p95 validation.
SafetyAlert mechanism is reliable and has authority to prevent production release if validation fails.
Vehicle module has access to real-time sensor data (payload mass, terrain classification, ambient conditions) to parameterize simulation.
p95 is a statistically valid metric for cycle time prediction under the duty cycle and site conditions specified.
open_risks: Model fidelity risk: Simulation results depend on external terrain and payload models. Version mismatch or model drift post-release could invalidate p95 predictions. Mitigation: implement version-locking protocol and continuous monitoring.

Input validation risk: Incomplete or incorrect site-condition inputs from Tasking could compromise simulation validity. Mitigation: add explicit input validation schema to Vehicle; reject out-of-bounds inputs with diagnostic SafetyAlert.

Statistical edge-case risk: Pre-production simulation may not capture all edge cases (extreme terrain, payload distribution outliers). Mitigation: implement continuous Telemetry monitoring post-release; trigger re-validation if observed p95 drifts above CT_target.

Module complexity risk: Vehicle module may become large if it owns phase decomposition, simulation, validation, input validation, and monitoring. Mitigation: decompose Vehicle into sub-modules (CycleController, DutyCycleSimulator, ValidationGate, InputValidator) with clear internal interfaces.

Ownership ambiguity risk: Current DP does not explicitly assign validation gating (DP5b) to Safety Services. Mitigation: refine DP to separate Vehicle-owned simulation (DP5a) from Safety-Services-owned validation gate (DP5b).
evidence_needed: Duty cycle simulation model: specification of phase decomposition logic, parameterization by site conditions, and p95 calculation method.
Input validation schema: formal specification of required site-condition inputs, acceptable ranges, and rejection criteria.
Model version-locking protocol: procedure for locking terrain and payload models to production releases; traceability mechanism in Telemetry output.
Continuous monitoring strategy: definition of Telemetry metrics (observed p95, phase durations, payload mass, terrain classification), monitoring frequency, and re-validation trigger thresholds.
Sub-module decomposition design: internal interfaces between CycleController, DutyCycleSimulator, ValidationGate, and InputValidator; ownership and responsibility matrix.
SafetyAlert integration: specification of validation failure scenarios and corresponding SafetyAlert messages; authority and enforcement mechanism.
Pre-production validation plan: duty cycle simulation under representative site conditions; acceptance criteria and sign-off procedure.
